title: Self-Driven Project â€” SAE-style autonomy levels for the whole SDLC, the value/risk-as-ODD dial, and tool-agnostic everything-as-code
summary: Prior-art survey grounding the framing decision #665 (epic #666): the self-driving-car model applied to the whole software lifecycle â€” a human sets the goal + a tolerance/risk envelope, the system drives the steps gated/transparent/reversible, escalating only on tolerance breach. THREE findings reshape the forks. (1) SAE-levels-for-the-SDLC is ALREADY published convergently â€” ASDLC.io (L1 Assistiveâ†’L5 Full, borrowing SAE's own 'Conditional/High/Full' names), Dash0's six levels (with an explicit L3.5 'Selective Auto-Merge'), Tessl, and arXiv 2509.06216 which explicitly calls for 'a hierarchical framework analogous to the SAE Levels'. So the mapping is legitimate prior art to CITE, not originate; the ASDLC/Dash0 ladders number by a review/merge-gating axis (proposeâ†’human-reviewâ†’auto-reviewâ†’auto-mergeâ†’live) that lines up with the repo's own dev-browser ladder (#141: report-onlyâ†’proposeâ†’live-verifyâ†’open-PR), whereas Swarmia numbers by agent-topology (singleâ†’swarm) and is NOT comparable. Notably NONE of the published ladders has an L0 'report-only' floor â€” that is a genuine addition. (2) The GENUINELY NOVEL contribution (not found in any surveyed standard/framework): partitioning EVERY lifecycle activity into value-creation vs risk-mitigation and using the risk side â€” described by named quality dimensions â€” as the per-activity AI-autonomy DIAL (the dimensions act as the SAE Operational Design Domain / tolerance envelope governing how far the AI drives each step). The substrate it stands on is precedented and citable: the dimensions map to ISO/IEC 25010:2023 (revised 8â†’9 characteristics, adding 'safety'; usabilityâ†’interaction-capability, portabilityâ†’flexibility; ISO calls them 'quality requirements', not NFRs), and the dimension list is best modeled as an OPEN, extensible registry with a default flavor (Config-Extends-Platform-Default) â€” strong precedent in FURPS+ (the '+' is literally an extension slot), SEI/ATAM (open quality-attribute scenarios, a set that grows edition to edition) and arc42 (a 184-quality default catalog tailored into a per-project quality tree); ISO's top level is fixed-per-revision but applied by selection/tailoring. (3) The methodology must be codified SEPARATELY from any tool we ship, as tool-agnostic everything-as-code, so a third-party project-management/CI tool could read and drive the SAME artefacts (no lock-in; our tools are non-blocking consumers). This is well-precedented as a pattern â€” GitOps names it ('declarative desired state in Git', OpenGitOps Principles v1.0.0: declarative/versioned-immutable/pulled/continuously-reconciled), and per-category neutral schemas prove the play (SARIF/OASIS for findings, SPDX/ISO-5962 & CycloneDX/OWASP for SBOM, OpenTelemetry/OPA/CloudEvents/CNCF, OSCAL/NIST for compliance-as-code) â€” but work-tracking/project-management data is a genuine WHITE SPACE: there is no adopted open work-item interchange standard (portability is lossy CSV, non-portable even GitLab CEâ†”EE), and the repo's own backlog-as-version-controlled-Markdown is docs-as-code applied to that gap. Naming caveats: 'self-driving' is strongly Oracle's (self-driving database) so lean on 'self-driven' and avoid 'self-driving database/infra/SDLC'; 'agentic SDLC' is the crowded generic category; and 'Project' already names a WE standard entity (projects.json), so the brand overloads an existing term â€” a sub-decision in #665 Fork 3. Classification rulings the survey settles: the autonomy ladder is a configured level (already ratified for the dev-loop in #141, default open-PR) not a new fork; the tool-agnostic artefact contract legitimately IS a Protocol (the one place a third-party tool must swap in â€” minimize-lock-in's single escapable lock); the dimensions are an open registry not a closed five; the non-technical control plane is plateau-app product. Full grounding in reports/2026-06-15-self-driven-project-prior-art.md.
